Web search found no documented kayak rescue incident at coordinates 50.775°N, 1.300°W with matching parameters. Location is real (Solent, UK) but scenario is training exercise.

privileged_info_summary: The SAR Drift Modeling task is based on a hypothetical kayak rescue scenario set in the Solent (50.775°N, 1.300°W), a real maritime area near Southampton, England. All incident parameters are realistic and verified: Last Known Position is 50°46.5'N 001°18.0'W; wind conditions are 20 knots from 315° (North-West, Force 5); current sets 100° at 1.5 knots (typical for Solent tidal streams); leeway factor is 3% of wind speed (consistent with SAR practice for certain objects); search duration is 2 hours. The task uses Bridge Command, an open-source maritime simulator. Ground truth datum position calculated from vector mathematics is 50.752175°N, 1.199794°W, with 0.5 nautical mile tolerance for verification.
